purpose: A reproducible, human-followable product-demo language shared by the final manual and the approved reusable production skill.

camera.useFocusWhen: The interaction target occupies less than roughly 40% of the frame or its label must be read.
camera.returnToOverviewWhen: The result spans panes, windows, or a changed spatial relationship.

referenceAnalysis.lesson: Keep focus when a small selection is the entire task.
Focus on the tab target, then return to overview to show the spatial result.
Use a close state for creation and an overview for the opened terminal.
A context-changing workflow may begin wide, focus on the restored state, and end readable.
Use focus for status text and overview for relationships among multiple sessions.

paneLayout.goal: Move beta.md to the right of alpha.md and confirm the resulting split.
paneLayout.input: Two Markdown files are open as tabs in one document pane.
paneLayout.action: Grab beta.md, drag it to the broad right-edge drop region, inspect the half-pane preview, and release.
paneLayout.outcome: alpha.md and beta.md remain open in a left-right split with independent tab strips.

workbench.goal: Open README.md from the workspace home with Quick open.
workbench.input: A project is open and no document is active.
workbench.action: Open Quick open, type README, and select the matching document.
workbench.outcome: README.md opens in the document workbench with Preview visible.

guide-split.goal: Move beta.md to the right of alpha.md.
guide-split.input: Two Markdown files are open as tabs in one document pane.
guide-split.action: Drag beta.md to the broad right-edge drop region and release after inspecting the preview.
guide-split.outcome: Both documents remain visible in a left-right split.

split.goal: Move the Terminal Pane from below the document to its right.
split.input: A document and Terminal Pane are visible in a top-bottom layout.
split.action: Drag the terminal tab-bar surface into the broad right-side drop region.
split.outcome: The document and terminal settle into a left-right layout without losing the shell session.

search.goal: Find a phrase across the project and open its result.
search.input: README.md is open in the workbench.
search.action: Open project search, type a distinctive phrase, inspect the path and line, and select the result.
search.outcome: docs/review.md opens from the selected body-search result.

diff.goal: Review a changed Markdown document in rendered Diff.
diff.input: README.md has committed content plus two working-tree changes.
diff.action: Enable Diff and select the first entry in the Changes rail.
diff.outcome: Rendered changes and their matching Changes summary are visible together.

terminal.goal: Open a real shell session and run one project command.
terminal.input: README.md is open and the Terminal Pane is closed.
terminal.action: Reopen the Terminal Pane, create a session, and run printf.
terminal.outcome: The shell output appears in the terminal beside the project document.
